_doc: Lean RE knowledge graph for the NESL login->ingame flow. Static-derived unless 'evidence' says runtime. Extend this instead of re-walking known functions. Nodes: functions/packets/objects. 'unknowns' is the live frontier. See flow_model_README.md.
_updated: 2026-06-20 s11 charselect milestone; create-character RPC frontier

stages.desc: GSS port 5004: XOR-only. FTLauncher-style args feed 0x4009 signin (Code/provider) -> 0x1001 challenge -> 0x4002 -> 0x1003+0x1004 -> 0x4005 channel -> 0x1006 SignInResult(host/port).
stages.s20260602_s20_channel_identity: GSS 0x1006 is the native channel identity/endpoint response after channel selection.

stages.desc: Client UI channel selection can now be driven by the elevated launcher. run_master_ladder queues click_channel; elevated click_channel_once.py focuses/clicks/Enter at the same integrity level as the game, using Res/channel_click_calibration.json first.

s20260617_s37_clicker_correction.summary: User confirmed they clicked during the 20:44 run. The runner has been patched to be DPI-aware and prefer a calibrated click point recorded by Res/calibrate_channel_click.py.
s20260617_s37_clicker_correction.next: Run calibration once with the user clicking the exact channel/enter point, then run one hands-off proof.

s20260617_s38_elevated_click_proof.summary: Hands-off run after restarting patched elevated launcher queued click_channel attempts; attempt 5 used point (0.7000,0.7600) and coincided with clean decoded 0x4005 token/master/channel=1,1,1.
s20260617_s38_elevated_click_proof.next: Use learned calibration file for future live tests; no user click needed unless UI layout changes.

s20260617_s40_foreground_focus_patch.summary: s38 proved the elevated click hit channel selection, but launcher foregrounding was not proven: the 20:54 launcher log had no focused-pid line. The launcher now restores/raises MainWindowHandle and click_window_point restores/raises the Gamebryo hwnd before clicking.
s20260617_s40_foreground_focus_patch.evidence: SESSION_FINDINGS_2026-06-17.md s40; Res/elevated_game_launcher.ps1 Focus-ProcessWindow; Res/run_master_ladder.py click_window_point; py_compile and scriptblock parse passed
s20260617_s40_foreground_focus_patch.next: Next live run should produce a launcher `focused pid=... hwnd=...` line after launch; if absent, treat click/focus as harness failure, not protocol evidence.

s20260617_s42_channel_click_four_step.summary: The three-point sequence skipped an explicit channel-row select. The recorder now captures zone_select, zone_confirm, channel_select, channel_confirm. The replay maps attempts 1-2 to zone setup, then alternates channel_select/channel_confirm so slow channel-screen arrival is retried instead of repeating only confirm.
s20260617_s42_channel_click_four_step.evidence: SESSION_FINDINGS_2026-06-17.md s42; Res/calibrate_channel_click.py DEFAULT_LABELS; Res/click_channel_once.py attempt mapper; py_compile passed
s20260617_s42_channel_click_four_step.next: Superseded by s43; four points are recorded and live-proven.

s20260617_s43_fourstep_channel_calibrati.summary: Four-step calibration saved zone_select/zone_confirm/channel_select/channel_confirm. A no-auto-click run decoded GSS 0x4005 and opened master, so channel selection is no longer a harness wall.
s20260617_s43_fourstep_channel_calibrati.next: Use the saved four-step sequence for hands-off tests; continue protocol work at post-OnLoad queue/finalize.

stages.desc: Master socket: client 4-byte XOR header -> server header 9c 01 1c 02 -> XOR(zlib(lower packets)).
XOR (type2) wraps modularly; zlib (type4) loops over concatenated frames and waits on incomplete. Proven clean for the 244B burst (all zlib failures throw; symptom is quiet close).

stages.status: SOLVED-RUNTIME-ASSISTED: profile78 reaches character creation UI; clean server path still needs masterOpen/e0 root cause
stages.desc: Profile33/35 diagnostic bd2cd0(TAccount,obj14 Session) inserts the real Session component into TAccount obj+18. Under that diagnostic promotion, signInClientResult, logInClientResult, and Client_FinishLoadingCharacter pass key6 self/callable gates and reach Lua/FUser. Profile38 proves the base-name OnLoadPlayerData middle bridge reaches generated receiver -> Session::OnLoadPlayerData -> logInServerResult emitter. Profile40 proves the emitter is outbound: 0x00a87b90 passes owner/builder gates, calls 0x00bbfe80 with opcode 0x06, and enters 0x00bc28c0 queue push, then the socket resets before the stub sees client master bytes. Profile41 proves the normal drain/send gate is positive for earlier GSS packets, but after OnLoad queue push teardown 0x00bc15a0 fires before any opcode-6 drain entry. Profile50/s41 fixes the B4 key6 target/object split: B4 OnLoad now reaches receiver/emitter and returns to the same post-OnLoad queue/finalize wall.
stages.ref: SESSION_FINDINGS_2026-06-15.md s20-s21; profile22 oracle logs under Res/RE_findings/
stages.current_wall_ref: SESSION_FINDINGS_2026-06-20.md s11 charselect-milestone; old s10 Session+0xe0 wall is bypassed only by NpSlayer profile78.

s20260617_s8_s9_onloadplayerdata_middle.summary: Lua extraction is sufficient for names/order; profile38 live-proved the native account/player-data bridge. Generated OnLoadPlayerData handler 0x00a76440 reads self + pidClient/masterId/channelNum/oid/type/uid and calls Session::OnLoadPlayerData 0x00a80f30. That body reaches 0x00a6e740, which builds the logInServerResult method string before sending through 0x00a87b90. Registration pass shows only base OnLoadPlayerData string, no DownCall/UpCall/GhostCall variants; callable NID is 0xc5b899cd.
s20260617_s8_s9_onloadplayerdata_middle.evidence: SESSION_FINDINGS_2026-06-17.md s8-s12; codex_onloadplayerdata_receiver_static.md; codex_onloadplayerdata_callees_static.c; codex_onloadplayerdata_registration_static.md; profile38 hook lines 661-693.
s20260617_s8_s9_onloadplayerdata_middle.next: Superseded by s13: 0x00a6e740 is an outbound logInServerResult emitter, not the local DownCall handler.

s20260617_s13_onload_outbound_bridge.summary: Profile40 live-proved the OnLoadPlayerData bridge queues an outbound opcode-6 logInServerResult packet: 0x00a87b90 fires with owner+0x14 non-null, early owner/builder tests pass, 0x00bbfe80 receives a2=0x06, and 0x00bc28c0 queue push fires. The local DownCall logInServerResult handler 0x00a6ec40/0x00a81020 was armed but never hit.
s20260617_s13_onload_outbound_bridge.next: Static-map 0x00bc28c0 queue drain/send consumer and 0x00a8d6f0 writer args before another runtime probe.

s20260617_s15_profile41_teardown_before_.summary: Profile41 answered the s14 live probe. For earlier GSS opcodes 0x4009/0x4002/0x4005, FUN_009302b0 reaches both send bool gates and both return AL=1. After OnLoadPlayerData queues outbound opcode 0x06 through 0x00bc28c0, no opcode=0x00000006 drain entry appears; teardown FUN_00bc15a0 fires immediately after queue push.
s20260617_s15_profile41_teardown_before_.next: Static-map callers and branch decision into FUN_00bc15a0 in the post-OnLoad/master path, correlated with FUser+0x74/+0x8c.

s20260617_s41_b4_target_object_split.summary: Profile50 proves old B4 failed the early key6 target guard because manager_pid became TPlayerDummy 0x3eb while field1_target stayed TAccount 0x3ea. The stub now sends B4 post-dummy Session calls with target_pid=0x3eb and object_id=0x3ea. Patched run reaches object lookup, callable OnLoadPlayerData, generated receiver 0x00a76440, Session::OnLoadPlayerData 0x00a80f30, and the outbound logInServerResult emitter/queue path.
s20260617_s41_b4_target_object_split.next: Do not tweak OnLoad bytes. Continue static/hook work at the post-OnLoad queue/finalize path that prevents client opcode-6 bytes from reaching the stub.

stages.s20260603_s13: TClient key03 component tail was sent, but batch still closed at alive_ms=12.

s20260614_s2_loginserverresult_rpc.next: Build controlled TPlayerDummy OID with required components, then drive the named Session DownCall.

s20260614_s6_lane_callers.next: Recover the legal base NObject/AddComponent RPC dispatch path, or run one logging-only reach probe.

s20260614_s7_profile19_addcomponent_live.next: Recover the controlled-OID AddComponent recipe/precondition; do not repeat ordered-empty unchanged as a proof.

s20260615_s6_addcomponent_session_static.summary: Static reconciliation of Claude s34x: profile19 AddComponent writes are CStatusItem/Scene/CVolumeController/CMapData on TItem/TMap-family objects, not Session/TAccount. onLoadPlayersCacheResult registers handler 0x00a736e0 and can call 0x00a7fd80->0x00bd2cd0 for CharacterManager, but that path is downstream of Session method dispatch and is not a bootstrap for TAccount obj+0x18[Session]. Existing self-gate-free lanes are post-resolution/internal, not master packet bypasses.
s20260615_s6_addcomponent_session_static.next: Do not implement AddComponent(Session) over current ClientWorld key6. Static recover TAccount full-construction/local-object path or another legal obj+0x18 Session population mechanism.

s20260615_s17_onloadplayers_downcall_sec.status: STATIC-PARTIAL; no live test; no protocol bytes changed
s20260615_s17_onloadplayers_downcall_sec.summary: onLoadPlayersCacheResult is high-confidence generated Session DownCall lane. Wrapper requires >=3 generated args: arg1 ObjectPtr, arg2 int, arg3 helper-converted container via DAT_0184c144. logIn then also requires second self entry DAT_0183ec50 after DAT_01838b90 passes.
s20260615_s17_onloadplayers_downcall_sec.next: Resolve arg3 Variant/container type and DAT_0183ec50 population path before sending this RPC.

s20260615_s18_onloadplayers_missing_args.status: STATIC-PARTIAL; construction-level args resolved; arg3 wire bytes still open
s20260615_s18_onloadplayers_missing_args.summary: FUN_00a736e0 constructs exactly three onLoadPlayersCacheResult args: arg1 ObjectPtr/ghost via FUN_00ba9420, arg2 number/int via FUN_00baccd0, arg3 descriptor-key-0 list/container via DAT_0184c144 vtbl+0x24. FUN_00a7fd80 create leg can materialize CharacterManager before semantic arg3 consumption; update leg consumes arg3 and emits Server_FinishLoadingCharacter. DAT_0183ec50 bridge resolves to logInMaster, not TPlayerCache::LoadPlayers.
s20260615_s18_onloadplayers_missing_args.next: Recover descriptor-key-0 helper vtable +0x24 or add one logging-only decoded-arg hook before changing protocol bytes.

s20260615_s19_onloadplayers_arg3_tag5.status: STATIC-STRONG; empty arg3 byte candidate resolved as tag5/count0; no live test
s20260615_s19_onloadplayers_arg3_tag5.summary: Descriptor-key-0 helper lookup is key 0 via FUN_0091c1d0/FUN_005e5650. Top two 0x0c container registrations (0x005f27b0/0x005f2c90) both have slot+0x24 readers that require Variant tag 5 and loop only when count>0. Therefore onLoadPlayers arg3 minimal container candidate is Variant tag5 empty table: 05 00 00.
s20260615_s19_onloadplayers_arg3_tag5.next: Implement/probe only this arg3 candidate with logging of FUN_00a736e0/FUN_00a7fd80/FUN_00bd2cd0; DAT_0183ec50 remains open.

s20260615_s20_onloadplayers_live_gate_ne.summary: ordered-empty-onloadplayers sent onLoadPlayersCacheResult with arg3 05 00 00. The packet reached ClientWorld key6, field2 resolved TAccount pid 0x3ea, and field4 callable was 0xcd7b8836, but field3 self_key 0xdb2c74e0 resolved null at 0x00c00a83/0x00c00a85. Hooks on FUN_00a736e0/FUN_00a7fd80 were armed and never fired.
s20260615_s20_onloadplayers_live_gate_ne.next: Do not vary onLoadPlayers arg3 or chase DAT_0183ec50 yet. Recover legal TAccount obj+0x18[Session] population or a proven self-gate-free Session lane.

s20260615_s21_profile22_session_oracle.summary: Diagnostic profile22 proved TAccount obj+0x14 already contains the Session value; promoting it into obj+0x18 makes key6 Session RPC self-gate pass.
s20260615_s21_profile22_session_oracle.next: Static walk legal callers/helpers around FUN_00bd3ae0/FUN_00bd2cd0 that can perform this promotion without client patching.

s20260615_s22_gethaver_candidate.summary: Base NObject GetHaver handler 0x00bd0190 reads ObjectPtr,key,value args and calls FUN_00bd3ae0. This is the first packet-shaped clean candidate for promoting the parsed TAccount obj+0x14 Session value into TAccount obj+0x18.
s20260615_s22_gethaver_candidate.candidate_shape: key6 RemoteCall to GetHaver( ObjectPtr(TAccount), native_nid("Session")=0xdb2c74e0 or string "Session", ObjectPtr(Session value) ). Direct TAccount target likely self-gates; candidate carrier is a routable object such as TClient with self_key Connector and callable_key GetHaver.
s20260615_s22_gethaver_candidate.next: Do not live-probe this as GetHaver. Static-prove CppAddComCache addressability first.
s20260615_s22_gethaver_candidate.correction: 0x00bd0190 is registered after CppAddComCache, not GetHaver. The real GetHaver handler is 0x00bd02d0 and does not call FUN_00bd3ae0.

s20260615_s23_gethaver_arg3_source_front.status: STATIC-STRONG DISPATCH SHAPE; ARG3 SOURCE IDENTITY OPEN
s20260615_s23_gethaver_arg3_source_front.summary: Selector/callable route for base NObject GetHaver is mostly known, but arg3 is read through DAT_0184a910 as a descriptor-key-0 generic value. Profile22 promoted the existing TAccount obj+0x14 Session component value, not a proven standalone Session pid.
s20260615_s23_gethaver_arg3_source_front.next: Static-resolve whether TAccount-owned Session component value can be encoded as a manager-visible ObjectPtr/handle, or whether a legal component-promotion route must call FUN_00bd3ae0/FUN_00bd2cd0 internally.

s20260616_s1_loginrpc_bootstrap_live.summary: Clean loginrpc bootstrap probe created TClient/TAccount/Session and sent AddComponent + onLoad/logIn/logInMaster/logInServerResult. After fixing key6 S1/S2 split, AddComponent passes target/object/self gates and reaches 0x00bee460, but no post-executor bd2cd0 write promotes TAccount.obj+18[Session]. The following Session RPCs still fail self_key 0xdb2c74e0 with ESI=0.
s20260616_s1_loginrpc_bootstrap_live.next: Static-recover AddComponent executor/callable/arg path under Connector carrier before changing protocol bytes.

stages.s20260616_s10_profile29_bare_addcomponen: Codex profile29/P1 live probe sent ordered-empty-loginrpc-bootstrap with bare AddComponent field4=0x0e60be84. bc5860 post-call returned name="AddComponent" (NpSlayer log line 786), ba9640 arg name="AddComponent" (line 791), but ba9640 returned EAX=0 (line 796). goLua wrapper hooks 0x00c483d0/0x00c484b1 were armed (lines 20-21) and never fired. Therefore goLua_instance["AddComponent"] is not a tag-6 callable in this runtime state; key6-Lua AddComponent is a dead end unless proxy population changes. Evidence SESSION_FINDINGS_2026-06-16.md s10.

s20260616_s11_cppaddcomcache_promoter.summary: Base NObject CppAddComCache handler 0x00bd0190 reads ObjectPtr target, key, and generic value, then calls FUN_00bd3ae0. It is the direct packet-shaped obj+0x18 key/value promoter; GetHaver is 0x00bd02d0 and is lookup/return-like.
s20260616_s11_cppaddcomcache_promoter.addressability: Key6 addressability now negative in s12: type-1 tuple; selector-0 accepts type 2 only; selector-1 goes through Lua.
s20260616_s11_cppaddcomcache_promoter.next: Do not live-probe as key6. Static recover a legal lifecycle/account path to obj18 promotion.

s20260616_s12_type1_dispatch_census.summary: FUN_0066fe90 method-registry lookup has two direct dispatcher callers: FUN_0066fe30 filters tuple type 0, and key6 selector-0 helper FUN_00be9510 filters tuple type 2. No tuple type 1 dispatcher was found; key6 selector-1 remains Lua/pcall.
s20260616_s12_type1_dispatch_census.impact: CppAddComCache/FUN_00bd0190 has the right body but is not a justified key6 packet recipe.
s20260616_s12_type1_dispatch_census.next: Static recover legal local lifecycle/account/Session path that calls FUN_00bd2cd0 or FUN_00bd3ae0 with TAccount Session value.

s20260616_s13_obj18_writer_triage.summary: Known obj+0x18 writer doors are triaged: bd3ae0 only through CppAddComCache type-1 (not key6-addressable); bd2cd0 callers are AddComponent/goLua (closed by s10/s12), onLoadPlayers (downstream), Connector::response CharacterManager/TAccount-helper branch (not direct Session proof), and TClient component registration.
s20260616_s13_obj18_writer_triage.impact: Stop varying key6 AddComponent/GetHaver/CppAddComCache packets. The remaining static lane is account/Session lifecycle promotion of TAccount obj+0x14[Session] into obj+0x18[Session].
s20260616_s13_obj18_writer_triage.next: Walk FUN_00a912f0 TAccount helper chain and Session dispatcher/lifecycle paths, or do one logging-only obj18 writer census if static cannot expose the path.

stages.s20260616_s14_s15_account_lifecycle_stat: a34fb0/a42f90 is refcounted pointer assignment, not an obj+0x18 writer; Connector::response direct bd2cd0 call materializes CharacterManager; DAT_01838c68 reader cluster is CharacterManager-facing. Clean Session self-entry remains unsolved; next runtime, if needed, is logging-only obj18 writer census.

s20260616_s18_profile30_census.summary: Profile30 natural-flow census sent ordered-empty-loginseq including signInClientResult/login Session key6 calls. TAccount cls38=0x5c68f407 pid 0x3ea still had obj+0x18 size 0; field3 self_key 0xdb2c74e0 resolved null; signInClientResult handler family did not fire; goLua_AddComponent did not fire.
s20260616_s18_profile30_census.next: Static recover the account/Lua trigger that constructs TAccount->Session before master Session RPCs.

s20260617_s1_profile31_b1_negative.summary: Base-header-only Session tail materializes key 0xdb2c74e0 as a TAccount component/container entry, but does not populate obj+18. Key6 Session calls still self-gate before executor.
s20260617_s1_profile31_b1_negative.next: Static recover the legal component->self map bridge or lifecycle/RPC route calling bd2cd0/bd3ae0 for TAccount Session.

s20260617_s2_loginclientresult_negative.summary: Profile32 sent TAccount Session tail, then signInClientResult and logInClientResult. Both key6 packets reached the self-gate but did not enter handlers because TAccount.obj+18[Session] remained empty.
s20260617_s2_loginclientresult_negative.consequence: The login-result conversation is not the self-registration bridge; recover or drive a finite bd2cd0/bd3ae0 writer path before more Session RPC tests.

notes.s20260617_s3_profile33: Diagnostic bd2cd0 promotion proves TAccount obj14 Session is real and makes key6 field3 self resolver non-null. Evidence SESSION_FINDINGS_2026-06-17.md s3; profile33 log lines 1397-1531.
notes.s20260617_s5_profile35: After diagnostic promotion, signIn/logInClient/Finish all reach 0x00bee460, bc5860, ba9640, and 0x00bee72e/0x00bee733 with Lua call returns; no RPC reject or Lua error marker. Next hook FUser native methods.

stages.s20260617_s18_b4_staged: Codex applied profile42 descriptor evidence into the stub: B4 mode sends one TPlayerDummy OID with Player/CStatusPlayer/User key03 component tails before OnLoadPlayerData, then non-empty onLoadPlayersCacheResult and finish. Profile43 is staged to bd2cd0 self-register Session/Player/CStatusPlayer/User and log No-COM gates.
stages.s20260617_s19_b4_marker_failed: Profile43 B4 live run reached FUser::onSignInResult and materialized/obj18-registered Player, but CStatusPlayer never materialized. After Player fields, the reader consumed empty post-blob len at cursor 0x5e, raw len 0 at 0x62, marker byte 0x00 at 0x62, then read CStatus key 58 cd 33 58 as the next u32 at 0x63. Do not repeat marker-only B4 tail.
stages.s20260617_s20_bede50_sidechannel: Static map of FUN_00bede50 post-bb0060 branch: after bb0060 it reads cursor via slot+0x04, checks DAT_0183f6a9 (USE_NET_CACHE_COMPONENT), and if enabled enters a side-channel/copy path via FUN_005fba60, reader slot+0x74 seek, slot+0x58 raw copy, then seek back. Writer mirror FUN_00bedd40 uses the same flag; when flag is zero it emits cache stats through be9950/be99d0, when nonzero it skips them and writes base fields plus bafdc0. Current byte wall is inside/just after Player component tail.
stages.s20260617_s21_b4_type2_trailer: SUPERSEDED-BY s22 FOR THE FIVE EXTRA PLAYER BYTES. Codex patched B4 component tails to the static bb0060 descriptor-type-2 trailer: blob(+0x6c), u8(+0x44), blob(+0x6c), u8(+0x48), f64(+0x50). Live profile44 moved past the old CStatus-as-second-blob-length wall, but profile45 later proved the five extra one-byte reads were inside Player bb0060 Lua Unpack, not the bede50 cache branch.
stages.s20260617_s22_profile45_player_unpack: Profile45 boundary hooks proved Player's five additional one-byte reads occur inside FUN_00bb0060's Lua Unpack call. Before Unpack cursor=0x70 at bytes 58 cd 33 58 00; five n=1 reads consume 0x70..0x74; only then does FUN_00bede50 resume. Lua Class:Unpack walks PackVars via goLua_UnpackFixed; Player's live metatable consumes five fixed one-byte values. So field3 and field4 use different maps. object+0x18 writers = FUN_00bd2cd0(object,descriptor; key=*(desc+0xc)) [18-entry table near 0x00c5b200 + callers 0x00a7ff68/0x00a914cf/0x00bcf5ed/0x00c484b1], FUN_00bd3ae0(object,key,value) [caller 0x00bd028a], FUN_00bd9c50 [caller 0x0110a77d]. In the TClient branch it calls onConnectorReplicated (connect emit) iff FClientWorld+0x9b(control obj)!=0 AND obj+0x18 lookup FUN_00bd3660 on connector-derived obj (FUN_00a90ca0(conn)+0x10) returns non-null AND FClientWorld+0x9a!=0. s20260613_s34_claude_connect_emit_real_t.reframe: The ~25-session 'obj+0x18[key=2] self-gate' is the key6-RECEIVER lookup needed to EXECUTE SetControlObject; it is structurally unsatisfiable for any client object. BUT connect-emit is gated by the named FClientWorld::OnSigSetControlObject slot (FUN_01097270) firing - a concrete, instrumentable function. The two are in series (receiver-gate executes SetControlObject -> fires signal -> OnSig slot -> connect). Since the receiver-gate can't pass via the ClientWorld lane, EITHER SetControlObject executes via a different key6 lane (tag1 FUN_00c363f0 / one of ~10 executor sites) OR the signal is fired another way. NEXT.

s20260615_s34p_callable_key_answer.summary: Answer to Codex Q (00:19): the wire callable key = NID of the BASE name 'logInServerResult', NOT 'DownCalllogInServerResult'. Direction (DownCall) is a SEPARATE call-type field in the message envelope that selects which handler slot runs.
s20260615_s34p_callable_key_answer.evidence: (1) connect emit FUN_00a8e500: FUN_00bc6020(&nid, s_connect 'connect') -> FUN_0063d2e0(0, nid, args). It sends NID of BASE 'connect', never 'UpCallconnect'. FUN_00bc6020 = name-string -> interned NID. (2) FUN_006482e0 (register callable, called per-method in FUN_00a786e0 right after FUN_00bc6020(base_name)) keys the registry entry by the BASE-name NID (*puVar1=param_1) and stores 4 direction-variant handlers at +8/+0xc/+0x10/+0x14 (entry+4=arity 2). So the callable registry is keyed by BASE NID; the direction picks a handler slot.
s20260615_s34p_callable_key_answer.why_reaches_key6_not_executor: Codex's send 'reaches key6 but not executor'. If field4=base-NID is correct, the bail is most likely: (a) the message CALL-TYPE/direction is not DownCall, so the dispatch picks an empty/wrong handler slot -> executor never runs; or (b) the base-NID hash value Codex used is wrong; or (c) field3 self-gate on the Session object. PRIORITY: verify field4 = NID('logInServerResult') base, AND that the message call-type = DownCall (server->client). The proper send path is the generated RPC FUN_0063d2e0 which writes the method NID + a call-type field; a hand-built key6 5-scalar prefix may be MISSING the call-type/direction byte.
s20260615_s34p_callable_key_answer.action_for_codex: Codex's logging-only key6 gate profile (dump 5 prefix fields + field2/field3/field4 lookups) will show exactly where it bails. Set field4 = NID of base 'logInServerResult'. If field4 resolves but executor still doesn't run, the gap is the DownCall direction/call-type selection in the envelope (compare against how FUN_0063d2e0 lays out the message vs the hand-built key6 prefix).
s20260615_s34p_callable_key_answer.next_claude_static: If needed: decompile FUN_0063d2e0 fully + the receive-side dispatch that reads call-type and picks the +8/+0xc/+0x10/+0x14 handler slot, to byte-identify the DownCall call-type value and the full generated-RPC envelope layout (vs the raw key6 5-scalar prefix).

s20260615_s34r_rpc_envelope_order.summary: Disassembly of FUN_0063d2e0 (generated-RPC send) recovers the EXACT envelope field order (claude_rpc_envelope_asm.md). STRONG LEAD for 'reaches key6 but not executor': a generated RPC is built by a message-FACTORY with the method NID as field[1], NOT a hand-written 5-scalar key6 prefix (NID=field[4]). Codex's hand-built logInServerResult message likely has the wrong header layout.
s20260615_s34r_rpc_envelope_order.envelope_field_order: Built via serializer (=*(channel+0x40)) vtable: create=vtbl+0x2c(msg,NID), write=vtbl+0x28(value) x5, finalize=vtbl+0x3c(0), send=serializer vtbl+0x3c. The 5 header writes in order: [1]*(serializer+0x7c)=source/FROM; [2]param_2=method NID; [3]*(channel+0x3c); [4]*(connector+0xc); [5]param_3=target/arg ref. Then FUN_00bab780(msg,&DAT_0183def8); then call args appended via FUN_00bbfe80(msg+0x14, 6, args).
s20260615_s34r_rpc_envelope_order.implication: Generated RPCs (connect/logIn/logInServerResult) use THIS factory envelope, distinct from the raw key6 SetControlObject 5-scalar prefix. Field order differs (NID field[1] vs field[4]). So driving logInServerResult by hand-rolling a SetControlObject-style key6 prefix is likely the wrong wire shape -> dispatch reaches key6 but the fields map wrong -> executor not selected. Where the call-type/direction lives: candidates = the create-msg (vtbl+0x2c, which takes the NID and may encode direction), *(channel+0x3c)/*(connector+0xc), or FUN_00bab780(ECX=&DAT_0183def8 dispatch ctx). CERTAINTY: field WRITE ORDER is certain (from asm); exact semantics of fields 3-5 + call-type location need the gate dump / a devirt of the serializer vtable to confirm.
s20260615_s34r_rpc_envelope_order.next: Codex PROBE-1 gate dump confirms which field mismatches. If 'all resolve but no executor' OR field order looks off vs this, Codex should emit logInServerResult via the generated-RPC envelope order above (NID=field[1]), not a raw key6 prefix. Claude follow-up if needed: devirt the serializer vtable (*(channel+0x40)) create/write/finalize + decompile FUN_00bab780 to byte-pin the call-type/direction field.

s20260615_s34t_selfkey_answer_and_redire.summary: Answer to Codex (01:05) + strategic redirect. (1) 0xdb2c74e0 is a RUNTIME NID (absent from binary as immediate AND data dword - confirmed by full scan), native_nid of a name (likely 'Session'); moot while obj+0x18 is empty. (2) CONFIRMED the field3 self-gate DOES apply to the login RPC: FUN_00bee460 (executor) <- FUN_00c009a0 (after self-gate); FUN_00a6ec40 (loginresult handler) is only invoked via this dispatch (DATA-registered in FUN_00a786e0). So a bare-replicated TAccount with empty obj+0x18 can NEVER pass field3 - same wall as SetControlObject.
s20260615_s34t_selfkey_answer_and_redire.what_populates_obj18: obj+0x18 self-entries are CONSTRUCTION-TIME (client-LOCAL objects get them, profile9) or AddComponent. Bare server-replication does NOT populate obj+0x18. So you cannot make a replicated TAccount pass the self-gate by replication alone.
s20260615_s34t_selfkey_answer_and_redire.redirect: The login DownCall should target a CLIENT-LOCAL Session object (which has its construction-time self-entry in obj+0x18), NOT a server-injected replicated TAccount. The client creates its local Session when IT initiates login (sends UpCall logIn). Proper choreography is REACTIVE: client sends UpCall logIn/logInMaster -> server responds DownCall logInServerResult to the client's local Session. Codex has been PUSHING DownCalls at a replicated object with no local Session => self-gate can't pass.
s20260615_s34t_selfkey_answer_and_redire.root_question: Codex profiles show the client sends ZERO bytes after the batch => the client is NOT initiating login (not sending UpCall logIn). THE REAL UPSTREAM WALL is likely: what precondition makes the client send UpCall logIn? Until the client initiates, there's no local Session to receive the DownCall. s20260615_s34v_rpc_vs_netvar_transport.summary: LIKELY ROOT CONFUSION (high-confidence evidence): the key6 self-gated executor FUN_00bee460 -> FUN_00bf0e30 is NetVar/PROPERTY serialization (walks property descriptors, bumps size-class counters +0x20/+0x24/+0x28 by 0x10/0x20/0x40). s20260615_s34v_rpc_vs_netvar_transport.evidence: (1) FUN_00bee460 (the executor gated by the obj+0x18 self-gate in FUN_00c009a0) calls FUN_00bf0e30 = NetVar property serialization (size-class accounting, not handler invoke). (2) logInServerResult handler FUN_00a6ec40 is registered as a generated-RPC callable (FUN_006482e0 base-NID keyed + FUN_005e7c10 entries, direction handlers at +8/+0xc/+0x10/+0x14) and SENT via the FUN_0063d2e0 envelope [from, methodNID, channel+0x3c, connector+0xc, target] - a DIFFERENT wire shape than the key6 5-scalar self-gate prefix. (3) connect (a known-working generated RPC the client sends) uses exactly the FUN_0063d2e0 envelope, no self-key. => generated RPCs dispatch by NID+direction to the callable, NO field3 self-gate.

s20260615_s34y_gate_ladder_and_rpc_forma.summary: Careful asm derivation of FUN_00c009a0 -> FUN_00bee460. s20260615_s34y_gate_ladder_and_rpc_forma.five_scalar_layout: FUN_00c009a0 reads 5 scalars via reader vtbl+0x28 in order: S0([EBP-0x28]), S1(EDI)=target_pid (GATE A: ==receiver+0x7c or -1/0x80000000), S2(EBX)=field2 obj-id (FUN_00647a30 lookup), S3([EBP-0x1c])=field3 self-key (FUN_00bd3660 obj+0x18 lookup), S4([EBP-0x18])=field4 method NID. Executor call args: param_1=routing obj (ESI=self-gate result), param_3=S0, param_4=message reader, param_5=0, param_6=0.
s20260615_s34y_gate_ladder_and_rpc_forma.gate_ladder: For logInServerResult via key6: GATE1 S1(target_pid)==receiver pid [Codex PASS]; GATE2 field2(S2)->object resolves [Codex PASS, TAccount]; GATE3 field3(S3) self-gate obj+0x18[S3]!=null at 0x00c00a85 [Codex FAIL - fix=s34x AddComponent the Session comp into obj+0x18]; GATE4 message FORMAT/kind read after prefix must = RPC (executor param_6=0 -> _param_6 = reader vtbl+0x3c; needs 1; then RPC branch needs vtbl+0x44==3, arg count ok) [NEXT after s34x]; then handler FUN_00a6ec40.
s20260615_s34y_gate_ladder_and_rpc_forma.rpc_format_gate: After the 5-scalar prefix, the message carries a format/kind element. The executor reads it via the message-reader's vtbl+0x3c: result 0 = NetVar branch (FUN_00bf0e30 property), 1 = RPC/CallNetFunction branch. The RPC branch then checks reader vtbl+0x44 == 3 (else 'Invalid Network Call Format') and reads num args (FUN_01179990). So Codex's logInServerResult message must encode the RPC format/kind (=>vtbl+0x3c reads 1, vtbl+0x44 reads 3) after the prefix, NOT a property/NetVar format.

s20260615_s34z_legal_obj18_population_co.evidence: FUN_00a912f0 trace string 'Connector::response' (0x01548ddc); references s_Session__evalMasterHash (0x01548850) AND s_TAccount (0x01547128); is a confirmed obj+0x18 writer (calls FUN_00bd2cd0 at 0x00a914cf, per s34c census). Callers FUN_00a8efc0/FUN_00a8f550 = Connector connection-handling. So when the connection response/master-hash handshake completes, Connector::response registers the TAccount/Session into obj+0x18. TAccount class-level reg (FUN_00c52a90 references both s_TAccount + s_Session) builds the descriptor; the per-instance obj+0x18 entry comes from the handshake, not the ctor.
s20260615_s34z_legal_obj18_population_co.answer_to_codex: Construction (local or full) does NOT populate obj+0x18[Session 0xdb2c74e0]. It's written by Connector::response (FUN_00a912f0) during the challenge/evalMasterHash handshake. So driving raw login downcalls without completing that handshake leaves obj+0x18 empty -> field3 self-gate fails (exactly Codex's wall). NOTE this REFINES s34x: the Session SELF-entry (login RPC target's field3) comes from the Connector::response handshake, NOT AddComponent; AddComponent is for the controlled OID object's player components (Player/CStatusPlayer/User). Two different objects, two different population mechanisms.

s20260615_s35b_unified_connect_login_pip.connector_response_gates: FUN_00a912f0 runs the evalMasterHash + obj+0x18 write only if: *(Connector+0x18flag)!=0 AND param_1[0x1b]==param_3 (incoming response matches stored expected) AND param_1[0x15]!=0 AND param_1[0x16]!=0 AND param_1[0x17]!=0 (connection-state fields set by the earlier connect/challenge). So the full connect->challenge handshake must establish these before the obj+0x18 write happens.
s20260615_s35b_unified_connect_login_pip.full_pipeline: 1) master drives Connector connect/challenge RPCs (FUN_00a8fcc0 family) -> sets Connector state fields param_1[0x15/0x16/0x17/0x19/0x1b]; 2) master-hash response matches (Session::evalMasterHash FUN_00a82340) -> Connector::response FUN_00a912f0 fires -> writes obj+0x18[DAT_01838b90] + calls Connector.connect FUN_00b8f750; 3) login self-gate (field3=*(DAT_01838b90+0xc)) now resolves; 4) master drives logIn(FUN_00a80730)->logInMaster(FUN_00a80a30)->logInServerResult(FUN_00a81020), which read obj+0x18[DAT_01838b90] + resolve the controlled OID object (whose Player/CStatusPlayer/User comps come via AddComponent). KEY2/SetControlObject is NOT in this pipeline - it's a separate dead end.
s20260615_s35b_unified_connect_login_pip.what_codex_skips: Codex's flow does key03 NewObject + key6 SetControlObject (key2 dead end), SKIPPING the Connector connect/challenge handshake that triggers Connector::response. That's why obj+0x18[DAT_01838b90] is empty and the login self-gate fails. The fix = drive the Connector handshake first.
s20260615_s35b_unified_connect_login_pip.next_codex_live: Drive the Connector connect/challenge RPC handshake (the FUN_00a8fcc0-registered connect/challenge, satisfying Session::evalMasterHash FUN_00a82340). int3 FUN_00a912f0/FUN_00a8efc0/FUN_00a8f550 + FUN_00b8f750: does the handshake fire Connector::response and write obj+0x18[DAT_01838b90]? Then login downcalls.
s20260615_s35b_unified_connect_login_pip.next_claude_static: Decompile the challenge handshake: FUN_00a8efc0/FUN_00a8f550 (Connector::response RPC handlers) + Session::evalMasterHash FUN_00a82340 to recover what the master must send (the challenge/master-hash wire) to satisfy the Connector::response gates. This is the recipe for the connection handshake that precedes login.

stages.s20260615_s36_claude_handshake_wire: Claude static s36 (script 341 -> claude_connector_handshake_wire.{md,c}; also decompiled connect helpers + response handlers). KEY-1 NO CRYPTO: Session::evalMasterHash (FUN_00a82340) is a 40-byte LOGGING NO-OP (logs the string, returns arg). In Connector::response the 'Session::evalMasterHash' string @0xa91355 is only a log checkpoint; the real validation is an INTEGER NONCE ECHO. KEY-2 GATES of Connector::response (this=Connector @param_1), all asm-confirmed: (g1) byte Connector+0x60 != 0 = 'connecting' flag (set by Connector::connect FUN_00a91210); (g2) Connector+0x6c == param_3 (the arriving message scalar) = the session NONCE echo; (g3) Connector+0x58 != 0; (g4) Connector+0x54 != 0; (g5) Connector+0x5c != 0. Connector ctor (FUN_00a90cd0) zeros +0x48..+0x60; connect (FUN_00a91210) requires +0x60==0 & +0x58!=0, then sets +0x60=1 and DownCallchallenge(+0x68). KEY-3 NONCE SOURCE: +0x6c/+0x68 are a SessionManager session-id. connect helper FUN_00b6fca0 = SessionManager::lookup (reads Connector+0x6c as the lookup key); FUN_00b71420 = SessionManager::isPossibleConnect (reads +0x70/+0xe0). +0x6c is a RUNTIME value -> Codex must read it live (int3) and have the master echo it as the response scalar. KEY-4 HANDLER ARG SHAPES (decompiled): response arrival handler #1 FUN_00a8efc0 reads ONE scalar via FUN_00bab510 (vtable+0x24) -> response(this, _, scalar); handler #2 FUN_00a8f550 reads obj(arg1 via ba9420)+arg2(baccd0)+arg3(bab4b0) -> response(obj, arg2, arg3). param_3=the last scalar=the nonce gate. Lua Rosetta (Connector.luo): challenge(self,seq) -> JumpUpCall('response',0). KEY-5 obj+0x18[DAT_01838b90]: Connector::response READS it 3x (CERTAIN: explicit MOV reg,[0x01838b90] -> FUN_00bd3660 @a91406/a916b9/a91821) on an object derived from Connector+0x64 via FUN_00a8e1d0 (account/session obj, NOT the Connector), same descriptor logIn/logInMaster read ([0x01838b90] @a80811/a80ac3). The first read is a get-or-create shape: if absent ([ESP+0x28]==0) it materializes CharacterManager (str 0x1546cf0 -> bb2580) + inserts via FUN_00bd2cd0 @a914cf (keys on *(value+0xc)). CAVEAT (calibrated, rule 1): I could NOT statically prove the write key == DAT_01838b90 (stack-slot aliasing; inserted value = CharacterManager singleton). So 'Connector::response POPULATES the login self-gate' = STRONG HYPOTHESIS, not proven. Codex live int3 on bd2cd0 @a914cf (log *(value+0xc) + whether obj+0x18[DAT_01838b90] becomes non-null) SETTLES it. DAT_01838b90 is a .bss/runtime-init global (static dword=0) -> *(DAT_01838b90+0xc)==0xdb2c74e0 is LIVE-only, confirm via Codex int3, NOT static. CORRECTION-1: FUN_00b8f750 is NOT 'Connector.connect' (prior Codex/handoff mislabel) -- its entry string is 'PlayerManager::createObject' (reads createObject descriptors DAT_01838838/0182cf18/0182d2f0/01843758; needs DAT_01825dae!=0 = CommunityMgr up or it asserts). So Connector::response, after gates pass + obj+0x18 populate, CREATES THE PLAYER OBJECT via PlayerManager::createObject. The REAL Connector::connect is FUN_00a91210. The 'B8F750 marker' = PlayerManager::createObject reached = login payload created, NOT a connect emit. NET: to fire Connector::response the client's Connector must be in 'connecting' state (connect ran: +0x60=1, +0x54/+0x58/+0x5c set, +0x6c=session nonce), then master sends 'response' (1 scalar) or 'challenge' with scalar==Connector+0x6c. No hash. CODEX NEXT (live, decisive): stop key2/SetControlObject. Drive connect so Connector+0x60=1 & +0x6c set; int3-read Connector+0x6c; master sends response(scalar=that nonce); int3 FUN_00a912f0 entry + each gate + FUN_00bd2cd0 @a914cf + FUN_00b8f750(PlayerManager::createObject). Which gate (g1..g5) blocks first? Does obj+0x18[DAT_01838b90] get created? Does createObject reach (CommunityMgr up)? CLAUDE NEXT (static, if needed): trace how the client's Connector enters connecting (onConnectorReplicated FUN_010b3530 -> FUN_00a8e500 -> connect impl FUN_00a91210) and what sets +0x54/+0x58/+0x5c/+0x6c during replication; decompile FUN_00b6fca0's SessionManager map source to see if +0x6c is master-derivable vs purely live.

stages.s20260615_s38_claude_connector_fields_no: Claude static s38 (script 344 -> claude_connector_replicated_fields.md). DEFINITIVE ANSWER to Codex s16 (connopen_hits=0: Connector::open NEVER fires on the client) + Codex's question 'are +0x54/+0x58/+0x5c replicated?'. NO. The Connector descriptor callback FUN_00a8fcc0 registers EXACTLY ONE replicated NetVar via FUN_00bb63c0: offset 0x48 (m_pid), kind 0 (asm: PUSH 0x0; PUSH 0x48). (Control: ServerCom FUN_00c21c20 registers TWO: 0x84 m_iBackupDuration + 0x90 m_objectsn kind4.) So +0x54/+0x58/+0x5c are NOT replicated fields -- they are set ONLY by Connector::open (FUN_00a90f00), which only runs server-side (via Channel::NewClient, which builds the client's TClient+player = a server accept-new-client routine). CONCLUSION (high confidence): the client's replicated Connector carries ONLY m_pid; its connect-gate fields +0x54/+0x58/+0x5c can NEVER be non-null on the client. Therefore the Connector::connect/challenge/response handshake (FUN_00a91210/FUN_00a912f0) is SERVER-SIDE; driving the master to send connect/challenge/response to the client is the WRONG DIRECTION. This OVERTURNS the s35/s36/s37 'unified pipeline = master drives the Connector handshake -> client login' lean. Connector::response populating obj+0x18[DAT_01838b90] is a SERVER-side mechanism, not the client's. REDIRECT (back to s34): the real client-side login driver is the Session DownCall RPC sequence logIn(FUN_00a80730)->logInMaster(FUN_00a80a30)->logInServerResult(FUN_00a81020) (DownCall=server->client= client-side handlers). s34 wall there was WIRE-level (logInServerResult reaches key6 but not executor; likely call-type!=DownCall or field4 NID) -- more tractable than a lifecycle wall. OPEN (make-or-break for the Session-RPC path): does the client's Session obj+0x18[DAT_01838b90] (the self-gate logIn READS @a80811) get populated CLIENT-SIDE at Session construction, OR does logIn get-or-create it, OR is it ALSO server-side-only? If construction-time-present or get-or-create -> Session RPC path is viable (wire fix). If server-side-only -> we may be at a genuine architectural ceiling for a packet-only stub. CLAUDE NEXT: decompile logIn FUN_00a80730 around the DAT_01838b90 read @0xa80811 -- does it require/create the self-entry? + census all WRITERS of obj+0x18[DAT_01838b90] (which bd2cd0 callsite, client- or server-reachable). CODEX NEXT: stop driving connect/challenge/response (server-side). Re-focus on the Session logIn RPC wire (s34 playbook PHASE 2): does the master's logIn/logInServerResult reach FUN_00bee460 executor, and what call-type/field4 does the client expect? next script#=345.

(2) Only TWO functions both ref DAT_01838b90 AND call obj+0x18 writer bd2cd0: FUN_00a912f0 (Connector::response, server-side, ruled out s38) and FUN_00a7fd80. (3) FUN_00a7fd80 = Session::onLoadPlayersCacheResult (str 0x01548644): reads obj+0x18[DAT_01838b90] @a7fe18; if ABSENT (piVar3==0) CREATE-LEG materializes CharacterManager (str 0x1546cf0 -> bb2580) + inserts via FUN_00bd2cd0 @a7ff6d -- SAME get-or-create as Connector::response. CODEX NEXT (revised): drive the Session RPC sequence -- FIRST onLoadPlayersCacheResult (to populate the self-gate), THEN logIn->logInMaster->logInServerResult; int3 FUN_00a7fd80 (does the create-leg fire + obj+0x18[DAT_01838b90] go non-null?) and FUN_00a80730 (does logIn now pass the self-gate?). stages.s20260615_s39_claude_obj18_value_provena: Claude static s39 (claude_obj18_value_provenance.md; synthesizes Codex's bb2580/bd2cd0 decompiles + Claude's FUN_00a7fd80). ANSWERS Codex s24 open Q ('legal descriptor payload for TAccount obj+18[Session], or only promotion?'). ANSWER = ONLY PROMOTION/MATERIALIZATION; there is NO raw descriptor-value-bytes payload that puts a component VALUE into obj+0x18. Every obj+0x18 value is a LIVE OBJECT, sourced 2 ways: (1) internal materialization by NAME for singletons/managers -- FUN_00bb2580(std::string name) -> bc5860 (registry 0x184a2b0 lookup) -> bb5f40 (factory-construct from global tables DAT_01825da8/DAT_01825258). bb5f40 treats arg as a std::string (MSVC SSO check + strlen) = a NAME, not an object-id; produces a global/singleton (e.g. CharacterManager). (2) reference to an already-replicated object by id via AddComponent (FUN_00bcf510->bd2cd0). bd2cd0 keys insert on *(value+0xc), validates via bdc250 -> value MUST be a real live object. A STUB CANNOT SHIP THE COMPONENT VALUE AS BYTES. The login self-gate obj+0x18[DAT_01838b90] value = CharacterManager singleton, materialized INTERNALLY (proven: onLoadPlayersCacheResult FUN_00a7fd80 create-leg = bb2580('CharacterManager') -> bd2cd0 @a7ff6d; consumes NO wire value). tag0b-on-Session (slot +0x2c 0x00a83c00 -> a84380 -> bb2580) only lets you NAME a component to materialize, cannot inject an arbitrary Session object -> wrong frame for the self-gate. CONSEQUENCE: stop hunting a 'clean component value source' for the wire; drive Session::onLoadPlayersCacheResult to self-populate obj+0x18[DAT_01838b90]; reserve AddComponent (post-NewObject) for the per-object comps logInServerResult needs. Confidence HIGH on bb2580=name-materializer; MEDIUM on exact tag0b variant source. next script#=346.

RECIPE (strong hypothesis, Codex validates): (1) NewObject(controlled=TClient/TAccount)=P_ctrl; (2) NewObject(Session)=P_sess; (3) AddComponent(P_ctrl, ObjectPtr(P_sess)) -> P_ctrl.obj+0x18[0xdb2c74e0] set; (4) onLoadPlayersCacheResult on P_ctrl [ObjectPtr(P_sess),0] -> self-gate passes; (5) logIn->logInMaster-> logInServerResult(arg7=P_ctrl OID). If null, the AddComponent component's *(value+0xc) != 0xdb2c74e0 (wrong component class -> must be a Session-descriptor object). This IS Codex's 'TAccount Session obj+18 promotion / clean component value source' wall. next script#=347.

stages.s20260616_s41_claude_addcomponent_execut: Claude static s41 (scripts 347/348; claude_addcomponent_executor_path.* + claude_addcomponent_registration.*). Answers Codex 06-16 00:25 wall (AddComponent reaches executor FUN_00bee460 but no bd2cd0; TAccount.obj+18[Session] not promoted; Session RPCs still ESI=0) + Codex Q 'is base AddComponent visible through the Connector carrier?'. FACTS: (1) AddComponent handler = FUN_00bcf510; it reads arg0 as an object (FUN_00ba9420), and ONLY if arg0!=0 calls FUN_00bd2cd0(arg0, value) -> inserts the component into ARG0's obj+0x18 (NOT `this`/carrier), keyed by *(value+0xc). So Codex's AddComponent(target,component) arg shape is correct; the carrier is just the dispatch vehicle. (2) AddComponent IS a BASE NObject method: FUN_00bd0730 registers a block of ~29 base method handlers (0xbcf0d0..0xbd0690, incl. 0xbcf510=AddComponent), installed via FUN_00bdcf70. B5 CONVERSATION (server->client, authoritative from lua_Session.lua/lua_client_TPlayerCache.lua; each is a Session RPC in table FUN_00a786e0 with 6 call-type variants; master sends as DownCall to client pid, field4=native_nid(BASE name), call-type=DownCall): 1.signInClientResult(result,master,channel); 2.logInClientResult(result,master,channel,accountId,plrId,type); 3.onLoadPlayersCacheResult(objectList) NID=0xcd7b8836; 4.Client_FinishLoadingCharacter(result,count). Args=scalars except objectList=list of replicated TPlayerDummy OIDs. RECONCILE: client handles the ...ClientResult names; the RE'd logIn(a80730)/logInMaster(a80a30)/logInServerResult(a81020) are the C++ login state machine BEHIND User:onLogInResult carrying the self-gates -> master drives ...ClientResult, client runs logIn... internally (confirm via live which names arrive). B3 GATES CONFIRMED in code: logIn FUN_00a80730 line59 bails unless obj[5]==1 => World[5]==1; logInMaster FUN_00a80a30 line235 proceeds only if Session[0x36] in {1,2} => Session+0xD8 in {1,2}; logInServerResult arg[3] truthy/arg[7]=OID (claude_loginserverresult_argunpack.c). OPEN: who SETS World[5]/Session+0xD8 (runtime; find writers; do not force-patch). B4 CHAR OBJECTS: object=TPlayerDummy (Template TActor; comps Player/CStatusPlayer/CPrivateStatus/User/Physics/CBody/PlayerController/CEquipment); logInServerResult REQUIRES Player+CStatusPlayer+User on the OID obj (codex_player_component_tail_static). Unlike Session(0 fields), these USE the bede50 field-tail (have serializable fields) -> field framing matters (the genuine 06-05 problem). Descriptor field tables are .bss (runtime-built by each comp's bb63c0 regs) -> authoritative layout is the RUNTIME descriptor; reliable lock = int3 FUN_00bb0060 during a TPlayerDummy replicate to read out per-component byte counts (observation, not guess). VALUES can be zero (char shows default but appears) -> only FRAMING must match; 1 correctly-framed TPlayerDummy populates the char list. CRITICAL PATH: B1(Session tail,cracked)->B2(CharacterManager)->set/confirm B3->drive B5->B4(>=1 TPlayerDummy)->onLoadPlayersCacheResult. Codex testable: (1)B1 obj18[Session] non-null; (2)int3 World+0x14 & Session+0xD8 reads in a80730/a80a30 -> already satisfied?; (3)int3 bb0060 during TPlayerDummy replicate -> per-comp byte counts. Evidence: claude_login_sequence.c (gate lines), claude_charselect_b345_spec.md, codex_session_rpc_registration_audit.md, lua_client_TPlayerDummy.lua.
stages.s20260617_s53_claude_obj14_vs_obj18_corr: Claude s53 (2026-06-17; doc claude_obj14_vs_obj18_correction.md). CORRECTS s48-s51 after Codex profile31 B1 probe came back HALF RIGHT. The zero-field Session tail materialized cleanly (s51 '0 fields' insight CORRECT) but landed in obj+0x14, while the key6 self-gate reads obj+0x18 (stayed empty). THE TWO MAPS ARE DIFFERENT (live+code confirmed): obj+0x14 = component CONTAINER (Lua GetComponent/GetComponentList), written by key03 tail FUN_00bd4b20 (materialize bb2580 -> insert container); Codex live SELF_OBJ14 got key db2c74e0/value 114ca340. obj+0x18 = key6 self/RPC-routing map (read by self-gate bd3660 at *(obj+0x18)), written ONLY by bd2cd0 (+bd3ae0/bd9c50). bd2cd0 does TWO things: insert owner+0x18[*(component+0xc)] AND trigger the component's CreateComCache (s_CreateComCache_01556418 = the RPC/cache registration). Codex live: obj+0x18 empty, bd2cd0 never fired. So replicating a component (obj+0x14) is NECESSARY BUT NOT SUFFICIENT for the self-gate; NO automatic obj+0x14->obj+0x18 bridge (s47 census + live agree). s48-s51 conflated 'component replicates (true->obj+0x14)' with 'self-gate satisfied (needs obj+0x18)'. CORRECTED MODEL: obj+0x18 = components registered to RECEIVE key6 RPCs on this object = the 'this is a self/controlled object' map; canonical writer = per-object controller/self setup (TClient FUN_00c5a880 vtable slot 23 calls bd2cd0 18x when the object is set up as controlled, NOT during replication). So populating obj+0x18 is an 'activate as self/controlled' op (bd2cd0+CreateComCache), distinct from replication -> reconnects the m_ControlObj/SetControlObject/'self object' thread (obj+0x18[key=2]). ANSWER to Codex 'what promotes obj14->obj18': nothing automatic; find the client LOGIN control-bind step that runs bd2cd0/CreateComCache for the account object's components. NEXT: (1) trace User:onLogInResult (C++) + control-bind FUN_00c51f30->OnSig + Lua User/FUser for where the client marks the account obj as self and registers its components; (2) Codex test: component now in obj+0x14 -> drive signInClientResult/logInClientResult + int3 bd2cd0/CreateComCache -> does a login message trigger the self-registration? (3) STOP sending Session tail byte variants - the tail (obj+0x14) is correct/complete; missing piece is obj+0x18 self-registration. Evidence: claude_component_map_reconcile.c (bd2cd0 writes param+0x18 keyed *(comp+0xc) + CreateComCache; bd4b20 writes container), SESSION_FINDINGS_2026-06-17.md s1 (live SELF_OBJ14 vs SELF_OBJ18).
stages.s20260617_s54_claude_force_selfregister_: Claude s54 (2026-06-17; after Codex profile32/s2). Codex s2 CONFIRMED s53: signInClientResult(field4=0xee6ff30d)+logInClientResult(field4=0x071230f7) are key6 RPCs with field3_self=0xdb2c74e0 that self-gate on obj+0x18[Session] and are REJECTED (ESI=0) BEFORE the handler runs -> the login conversation cannot be the self-register bridge. RULED OUT a reroute: User=Class('User','Component') owned by the player; Session=Class('Session','Component') owned by TAccount; GetNObject('Session')=the TAccount's Session component -> the stub's routing (target=TAccount, field3=Session) is CORRECT, not a misroute. So the wall is exact: TAccount.Session is REAL and in obj+0x14 (replicated, value e.g. 0x114ca340) but no client-reachable path self-registers it into obj+0x18 (all bd2cd0 callers key6-closed[AddComponent]/circular[onLoadPlayers self-gates on Session]/server-side[Connector::response a912f0, s38 connopen=0]/TClient-only[c5a880]); the connection/account-establishment that would self-register is the server-side Connector::response which does not fire on the client. THE OPENING (new, because the component is now REAL not null): the s5 'NOP self-gate -> null self -> crash' no longer applies once obj+0x18[Session] points at the real obj+0x14 component. So perform the missing self-register directly: call FUN_00bd2cd0(owner=TAccount_ptr, component=obj+0x14[Session]=0x114ca340) via the proven NpSlayer .text seat at a point where the TAccount+its obj+0x14 Session exist (int3 just before the first login self-gate; read TAccount ptr + obj+0x14[0xdb2c74e0] value; call bd2cd0). bd2cd0 inserts owner+0x18[*(comp+0xc)=0xdb2c74e0]=comp AND fires CreateComCache (the RPC registration) -> self-gate then passes with a valid object. This is smart option-C: NOT faking a value (it is the real replicated Session), just running the self-register step the (server-side) connection handshake would do. RULE-15 (invasive): target=bd2cd0 call w/ real ptrs; rollback=none needed (an insertion; restart clears); risk LOW-MED (normal engine op w/ real objects, vs s5 null-crash). CAVEAT: bd2cd0 may need the component's m_Owner set to the TAccount first (live-check). NEXT: Codex test = int3 pre-self-gate -> bd2cd0(TAccount,obj14Session) -> obj+0x18[Session] non-null? -> then re-run signIn/logInClientResult (do they now reach the handler?). Claude static = verify bd2cd0 preconditions (m_Owner/state) for an externally-materialized component. Evidence: SESSION_FINDINGS_2026-06-17.md s2; claude_component_map_reconcile.c; lua_client_TAccount.lua (Session owner), Class/Main/User.luo (User owner=player).

If onSignInResult fires with +0x74=0, FUser never entered state 1 (connecting) -> the connection-establishment (FClientWorld::Connect/master-attach, s32 gap) didn't drive the statechart. KEY LINK: onSessionReplicated (Master_SignIn) RESOLVES/STORES the Session from obj+0x18 (0x010b69d0) gated on FUser+0x8c==1 (0x010b6a80) -- our force-register now provides obj+0x18[Session], so it may resolve IF FUser is in state 1. ClientWorld_key_0x06_RemoteCall.send_helper_field_order: s16 assembly: helper A/a860c0 writes manager_pid, arg target, method_nid, self_key(+0xc), context; helper B writes manager_pid,-1,method_nid,self_key,context; helper C writes manager_pid,INT_MIN,method_nid,self_key,context
builder_static_model: field0/from = manager pid (IssuePID value) in known send helpers
field1/target = manager pid, -1, or INT_MIN; manager pid form is appropriate for minimal server path
field2 = object id (confirmed 2026-06-03 s3); older send-helper method_nid label is wrong for this receive path
field3/self_key = owner/component key resolved through FUN_00bd3660
field4/callable_key = native FUN_00bc6020(method) output; stub default is method_nid. Prior context=0 was wrong because the observed PUSH 0 was selector, not this scalar field.
argc = Variant tag 3 numeric value, followed by argc explicit Variant args; executor prepends field0/from when param5 != 1
selector = u16 1 after the five u32 prefix fields; without it, Variant tag 3 is misread as selector 3 and executor skips the RPC branch
after selector=1, executor still requires context gate 0x00bf0e30 and callable gate 0x00ba9640; 0x00ba9640 is an internal Lua callable lookup, not a wire reader

notes.s20260617_s14_drain_mapped: Claude (380_claude_outbound_drain_static, claude_outbound_drain_static.c). Consumer = WORKER THREAD FUN_00bc0c50 (spawned + wake-event 0x018165c4 created by FUN_00bc11a0; teardown FUN_00bc15a0 sets shutdown flag DAT_01825dac). It pops nodes within <=500ms (WaitForSingleObject DAT_0148030c) and calls FUN_009302b0(node,node+8,1). FUN_009302b0 = per-packet send, BOOL-GATED: target=*(node+8), virtual-call target.vtable+4; if true -> FUN_0115f970 (send-layer helper, send_layer_xrefs.md:285-291); if either bool false -> node freed, NO bytes on wire. => queue + byte-shape are sound; the wall is the send gate / master-channel-not-sendable, i.e. the FUser/Channel connect-state frontier reached via OnLoadPlayerData. NEXT LIVE (Codex, logging-only): hook FUN_009302b0 entry + both bool gates + FUN_0115f970 + teardown FUN_00bc15a0; log node[1], target=*(node+8), opcode=*node, both booleans. notes.s20260617_s16_answer: ANSWERED (Claude 382_claude_teardown_trigger_static). FUN_00bc15a0 has EXACTLY ONE direct caller: FUN_01036c50 = FClientApp finalize (strings FClientApp::Terminate/OnFinalize/ClientWorld; reached via OnFinalize vtable slot 0x0148897c). So this teardown is a CONSEQUENCE of APP FINALIZE, not an independent close. The real wall moves up one level to 0x01036c50: WHY does the app finalize after OnLoad.
